name: Battery Pack Simulator
purpose: Simulate mission-critical battery packs with static and dynamic attributes for aerospace and EV applications.
businessGoal: Provide a reliable, configurable source of battery pack data for all downstream simulators, enabling development and testing without real hardware.
problemStatement: Developers and engineers need realistic battery pack data during the development phase but have no access to real hardware. The simulator bridges this gap by generating configurable, realistic battery pack profiles.

simulates: Battery pack identity attributes (BPAN, battery_id, serial number)
Static attributes (chemistry, nominal voltage, capacity, pack configuration)
Dynamic telemetry state (SOH, SOC, temperature, voltage, current)
Manufacturing metadata (date, factory, inspector)
Lifecycle status transitions (Active, Decommissioned, Under Maintenance)
Cycle count and simplified degradation curves
doesNotSimulate: Real electrochemical reactions or physics-based models
outputs: Battery pack objects as JSON array
assumptions: All data is synthetic and tagged simulated=true
SOH/SOC values follow simplified linear degradation
Chemistry types are from a fixed predefined set

functionalRequirements: Generate battery pack with unique BPAN (21-character alphanumeric code, BPAN-{CHEM}-{YEAR}-{COUNTRY}-{SERIAL})
Support chemistry types: LFP, NMC, NCA, LTO, Solid-State
Generate static attributes: manufacturer, nominal voltage (V), capacity (Ah), cell configuration (series × parallel)
Generate dynamic state: SOH (0–100%), SOC (0–100%), temperature (°C), pack voltage (V), current (A), cycle count
Support batch generation of 1–1000 packs per run
Export output as JSON and CSV
Tag all output with simulated=true, simulation_run_id, and generated_at timestamp
Support configurable random seed for reproducible outputs
nonFunctionalRequirements: Runs locally on developer machine, no cloud dependency
FastAPI REST API for programmatic access
All inputs validated with Pydantic v2 models
pytest test suite with minimum 70% coverage
No production or real device data used
technologyRequirements: Pydantic v2 (data validation and JSON Schema generation)
architectureNotes: CLI → BatteryPackGenerator (core) → JSON/CSV output. Streamlit wraps the generator with a form UI. FastAPI exposes /simulate, /health, /sample endpoints. Pydantic models ensure schema validation on all paths.
cliCommands: python -m sim_001 sample  # Print sample input JSON

apiEndpoints.description: Generate battery packs from input configuration
Health check and simulator version info
Return sample input JSON for reference
Return JSON Schema for input and output

testCases.name: Valid single pack generation
testCases.description: Generate one LFP pack and verify all required fields are present and within valid ranges

testCases.name: Batch generation of 100 packs
testCases.description: Generate 100 packs and verify unique BPANs, correct count, and metadata

testCases.name: Invalid chemistry type
testCases.description: Pass unknown chemistry 'XYZ' and verify validation error is returned

testCases.name: Seed reproducibility
testCases.description: Run twice with same seed and verify identical outputs

testCases.name: SOH boundary values
testCases.description: Generate packs at SOH=0%, SOH=100% and verify valid output

securityRequirements: All generated data must include simulated=true field
No real production battery data may be used as input
No secrets or API keys committed to repository
Input fields validated and bounded to prevent injection
Output files written to designated output directory only
manualVerificationSteps: Clone repository and run pip install -r requirements.txt
Run CLI: python -m sim_001 run --num-packs 5 --chemistry LFP
Verify 5 battery pack objects in output JSON with simulated=true
Run streamlit run app.py and verify UI loads with input form
Submit form and verify output table and JSON displayed
Call POST /simulate with sample JSON and verify 200 response
Run pytest and verify all tests pass with 70%+ coverage
definitionOfDone: All 8 functional requirements implemented and tested
pytest suite passing with ≥70% code coverage
README with setup, usage, and examples complete
QA review passed and Architect approval received

name: Battery Identity Simulator
purpose: Simulate trusted battery identity scenarios including valid certificates, expired credentials, forged identity, ownership transfer, and chain of custody.
businessGoal: Enable testing of identity verification logic and custody tracking without requiring real certificates or physical battery identity hardware.
problemStatement: Battery identity verification involves complex certificate chains, ownership records, and provenance metadata. Without a simulator, testing identity scenarios requires real infrastructure that is not available during development.

simulates: Valid battery identity with active certificate
Invalid or unknown battery identity
Expired certificate scenario
Forged or tampered certificate scenario
Ownership transfer records
Chain of custody history
Identity verification response (pass/fail/warn)
assumptions: Certificates are represented as simple signed JSON objects
Ownership is tracked via transfer records
limitations: Does not integrate with real PKI

functionalRequirements: Generate valid battery identity record with certificate metadata
Simulate expired certificate scenario with configurable expiry date
Simulate forged certificate with tampered fields flagged
Simulate unknown battery identity (not registered)
Generate ownership transfer record (from owner, to owner, reason, timestamp)
Build and return chain of custody history for a battery
Return identity verification result: TRUSTED, EXPIRED, FORGED, UNKNOWN
Produce identity audit event for each verification attempt
nonFunctionalRequirements: Runs locally, no cloud or PKI infrastructure required
Python 3.10+ with CLI and Streamlit UI
FastAPI REST API for integration testing
architectureNotes: Scenario selection → IdentityScenarioEngine → verification response + audit event. Scenarios are enum-driven. Streamlit allows scenario selection and parameter input. FastAPI exposes /verify-identity endpoint.
cliCommands: python -m sim_002 custody --battery-id BID-001  # Show custody chain

apiEndpoints.description: Verify battery identity for a given scenario
Simulate custody transfer and return updated chain
Return full custody chain for a battery
Health check

testCases.name: Valid identity verification
testCases.description: Verify a valid battery identity returns TRUSTED status

testCases.name: Expired certificate scenario
testCases.description: Expired certificate returns EXPIRED status with cert details

testCases.name: Forged certificate detection
testCases.description: Forged certificate is detected and returns FORGED with tampered fields

testCases.name: Custody transfer chain
testCases.description: Multiple transfers build a valid custody chain with correct chronological order

testCases.name: Unknown battery
testCases.description: Unregistered battery ID returns UNKNOWN status

securityRequirements: No real cryptographic keys or certificates used
All simulation data tagged simulated=true
Forged certificates must be clearly marked as SIMULATION
No real identity data used in any scenario
Input validation on all battery IDs and scenarios
manualVerificationSteps: Run CLI with valid scenario and verify TRUSTED response
Run CLI with expired scenario and verify EXPIRED response and certificate details
Run CLI with forged scenario and verify FORGED response with tampered fields
Launch Streamlit and test all 5 scenarios from the dropdown
Call POST /verify-identity API and verify JSON response
Run pytest and verify all tests pass
definitionOfDone: All 5 scenarios (valid, invalid, expired, forged, unknown) working
Custody transfer and chain retrieval functional

name: MQTT Telemetry Simulator
purpose: Generate simulated battery telemetry events as if originating from a BMS over MQTT, including attack and anomaly scenarios.
businessGoal: Allow telemetry trust and cybersecurity features to be developed and tested with realistic MQTT data streams without real BMS hardware.
problemStatement: Telemetry trust architecture and replay attack detection require realistic, configurable telemetry streams including anomalies and attack patterns. No real BMS is available during development.

simulates: Normal BMS telemetry event stream
Delayed telemetry (configurable delay seconds)
Duplicate packet injection
Out-of-range values (voltage, temperature, current)
Missing timestamp fields
Spoofed source identity
Replay attack (retransmission of old valid packets)
doesNotSimulate: Real MQTT broker (uses local file or HTTP simulation)
assumptions: MQTT is simulated via JSON output, not real broker
Packet timestamps are ISO 8601
limitations: Does not model BMS proprietary protocols

functionalRequirements: Generate normal telemetry events with valid timestamps and sequence numbers
Simulate delayed telemetry (configurable 1–300 second delay)
Generate duplicate packet pairs with same sequence number
Generate out-of-range values: voltage >60V, temperature >60°C, current >500A
Generate events with missing timestamp field
Generate events with spoofed battery_id (different from registered battery)
Generate replay events (copy of previous valid packet with updated sent_at but old timestamp)
Output MQTT topic, payload JSON, and QoS level for each event
nonFunctionalRequirements: Runs locally, no real MQTT broker required
architectureNotes: TelemetryScenarioFactory creates event streams per scenario. Events are published to a simulated topic namespace. Streamlit shows event stream in real time. FastAPI exposes /generate-events endpoint.

apiEndpoints.description: Generate telemetry event stream for a given scenario
List available telemetry scenarios
Health check
Return sample input JSON

testCases.name: Normal telemetry generation
testCases.description: Generate 10 normal events and verify valid timestamps, sequence numbers, and values

testCases.name: Replay attack scenario
testCases.description: Replay events have old timestamps flagged with REPLAY_DETECTED anomaly

testCases.name: Duplicate packet detection
testCases.description: Duplicate events share sequence number and are flagged DUPLICATE

testCases.name: Out-of-range values
testCases.description: Out-of-range events have voltage >60V and anomaly flag OUT_OF_RANGE

testCases.name: Missing timestamp
testCases.description: Events with missing timestamp field are flagged MISSING_TIMESTAMP

securityRequirements: All events tagged simulated=true — never mimic real device IDs
Replay events must be clearly marked as SIMULATION in anomaly field
No real network traffic generated
Spoofed battery IDs use reserved prefix SIM- to avoid collision with real devices
Attack scenarios documented as educational only
manualVerificationSteps: Run normal scenario and verify 10 events with valid timestamps
Run replay scenario and verify REPLAY_DETECTED anomaly on relevant events
Run out-of-range scenario and verify anomaly flags on voltage/temperature events
Launch Streamlit and verify event stream display per scenario
Call POST /generate-events API and verify JSON response
Run pytest and verify all test cases pass
definitionOfDone: Anomaly flags correctly assigned for each scenario
Streamlit UI showing event stream with scenario selector

name: Trust Score Simulator
purpose: Calculate battery mission trust score from identity, telemetry, firmware, cybersecurity, health, safety, and maintenance inputs.
businessGoal: Provide a quantifiable, auditable trust score that determines mission readiness of a battery pack for aerospace operations.
problemStatement: Mission-critical battery deployment decisions need an objective, multi-factor trust score. Without a simulator, validating the trust model requires full system integration.

simulates: Seven-factor trust score calculation with configurable weights
Trust readiness band assignment (Mission Ready to Grounded)
Factor-level breakdown with contributing score and weight
Score scenarios: high trust, medium trust, low trust, grounded
Score history simulation for a battery across multiple evaluations
assumptions: Factor weights sum to 100%
All inputs are simulated values
limitations: Does not account for temporal degradation trends

functionalRequirements: Accept seven factor scores: Identity Trust (15%), Ownership & CoC (10%), Config & Firmware Trust (15%), Telemetry Integrity (15%), Cybersecurity Risk (15%), Safety & Health Condition (20%), Maintenance & Mission History (10%)
Calculate weighted composite trust score (0–100)
Assign readiness band: Mission Ready (90–100), Ready with Caution (75–89), Engineering Review Required (60–74), Not Mission Ready (40–59), Grounded / Quarantine (0–39)
Return factor-level breakdown showing individual contribution
Support scenario presets: perfect, high, medium, low, grounded
Support custom weight override for research and experimentation
Generate trust score audit event with timestamp and run ID
nonFunctionalRequirements: CLI with preset scenarios and custom input modes
Streamlit dashboard with visual score breakdown
architectureNotes: TrustScoreEngine receives factor dict → applies weights → computes composite score → assigns readiness band → returns TrustScoreResult. Streamlit renders a gauge chart and factor breakdown table.

sampleOutputJson.recommendation: Battery is conditionally mission-ready. Review Cybersecurity Risk and Telemetry Integrity before deployment.

cliCommands: python -m sim_004 bands  # Print readiness band definitions

apiEndpoints.description: Calculate trust score from factor inputs
List preset scenarios with factor values
Return readiness band definitions and thresholds
Health check

testCases.name: Mission Ready score
testCases.description: All factors at 95 yield overall score ≥90 and MISSION_READY band

testCases.name: Grounded score
testCases.description: All factors at 20 yield score ≤39 and GROUNDED band

testCases.name: Weight sum validation
testCases.description: Custom weights that don't sum to 100 return validation error

testCases.name: Factor out of range
testCases.description: Factor score > 100 or < 0 returns validation error

testCases.name: Band boundary test
testCases.description: Score of exactly 75, 60, 40, 90 each assigned to correct readiness band

securityRequirements: Trust scores are simulated only, not to be used for real mission decisions
All outputs tagged simulated=true
No real telemetry or identity data used as input
Score calculations are transparent and auditable
manualVerificationSteps: Run CLI with --scenario high and verify score ≥75 and CAUTION or READY band
Run CLI with --scenario grounded and verify score ≤39 and GROUNDED band
Launch Streamlit and verify gauge chart reflects calculated score
Verify factor breakdown table shows correct weights and contributions
Call POST /score API and verify all fields in response
Run pytest and verify boundary tests pass
definitionOfDone: Seven-factor model implemented with correct weights
All five readiness bands correctly assigned by score range
Streamlit gauge chart and factor breakdown UI functional

name: Cyber Attack Simulator
purpose: Simulate five critical cyber attack vectors against aerospace battery management systems for education and detection rule testing.
businessGoal: Enable cybersecurity engineers to understand, model, and build detection rules against the most critical battery system attack vectors without using real attack tools.
problemStatement: Cybersecurity rule development requires attack scenarios to test against. Real attack tools are dangerous and illegal. A safe simulation provides the same learning and testing capability.

simulates: Replay Attack: retransmission of previously captured valid MQTT packets
Telemetry Spoofing: injecting false battery state values
Certificate Forgery: generating invalid signed identity certificates
Firmware Injection: simulating unauthorized firmware metadata
BMS Intrusion: simulating unauthorized BMS access events
doesNotSimulate: Real malware or malicious code execution
assumptions: All attacks are simulated and educational only
No real network traffic is generated
limitations: Does not produce real attack capability
Simplified attack models for educational purposes

functionalRequirements: Simulate Replay Attack: return old telemetry packet with updated sent_at timestamp
Simulate Telemetry Spoofing: inject false SOH, SOC, or temperature values in event
Simulate Certificate Forgery: return identity cert with invalid signature field
Simulate Firmware Injection: return firmware metadata with unexpected hash and source
Simulate BMS Intrusion: return access event from unauthorized source IP/identity
Each attack returns: attack_type, severity, attack_payload, detection_indicators, recommended_rule_id
Support multi-attack sequence for integration testing
nonFunctionalRequirements: Purely educational and simulation — no real attack capability
CLI, Streamlit UI, and FastAPI supported
architectureNotes: AttackScenarioFactory selects scenario → generates attack payload → returns AttackEvent with detection_indicators. Streamlit shows attack details and recommended detection rules.
cliCommands: python -m sim_005 list-attacks  # List all available attack types

apiEndpoints.description: Simulate a specific cyber attack against a battery
List all 5 attack types with descriptions
Health check

testCases.name: Replay attack simulation
testCases.description: Replay attack returns correct payload with old timestamp and duplicate sequence number

testCases.name: Telemetry spoofing simulation
testCases.description: Spoofed event has physically impossible values and SPOOFED flag

testCases.name: All 5 attack types available
testCases.description: GET /attack-types returns all 5 attack types

testCases.name: Detection indicators present
testCases.description: Each attack response includes at least one detection indicator

testCases.name: SIMULATED_ATTACK classification
testCases.description: All attack outputs include SIMULATED_ATTACK classification field

securityRequirements: All attack outputs must include SIMULATED_ATTACK — EDUCATIONAL ONLY classification
No real network connections, exploits, or malicious code
Simulated attack payloads must be structurally distinct from real attack tools
No real credentials, keys, or firmware used in simulation
Repository must include DISCLAIMER file on educational purpose
manualVerificationSteps: Run replay attack CLI and verify SIMULATED_ATTACK classification
Run all 5 attack types and verify each returns detection_indicators
Launch Streamlit and verify attack selector and output display
Call POST /simulate-attack API for each attack type and verify response
Run pytest and verify security test cases pass
definitionOfDone: All 5 attack types implemented with correct payloads and indicators

name: Detection Rule Simulator
purpose: Simulate detection rules and validate whether telemetry, identity, firmware, safety, or cyber events trigger warnings or hard-fail gates.
businessGoal: Enable the detection rule engine to be developed, tested, and validated against simulated events before integrating with real telemetry or production systems.
problemStatement: Detection rules must be validated against diverse event inputs. Without a rule simulator, testing detection logic requires coordinating multiple simulators simultaneously.

simulates: Rule evaluation engine for 8 predefined detection rules
Pass / Warning / Hard-Fail gate assignment per rule
Multi-rule evaluation against a single event
Rule override and bypass scenarios
Combined rule evaluation report
inputs: Event object (telemetry, identity, firmware, or cyber)
assumptions: Events are pre-generated by other simulators
Rules are stateless per evaluation
limitations: Simplified rule logic, not production SIEM quality

functionalRequirements: Implement Rule R-01: Replay attack detected (stale timestamp or duplicate sequence)
Implement Rule R-02: Missing telemetry timestamp
Implement Rule R-03: Invalid or expired certificate
Implement Rule R-04: Firmware hash mismatch or unexpected firmware version
Implement Rule R-05: Voltage out of safe operating range (<40V or >56V for 48V pack)
Implement Rule R-06: Critical temperature (>55°C or <-20°C)
Implement Rule R-07: Open cyber incident linked to battery
Implement Rule R-08: Physically impossible telemetry (SOC increase without charging)
nonFunctionalRequirements: Rules evaluable individually or in batch
architectureNotes: RuleEngine iterates over selected rules, evaluates each against the input event, and returns RuleEvaluationResult list. Gate decision (PASS / WARN / HARD_FAIL) is the worst result across all rules.
cliCommands: python -m sim_006 list-rules  # Print all rule definitions

apiEndpoints.description: Evaluate one or more rules against an event
List all available rules with descriptions and thresholds
Health check

testCases.name: R-02 missing timestamp HARD_FAIL
testCases.description: Event with null timestamp triggers R-02 HARD_FAIL

testCases.name: R-05 voltage out of range WARN
testCases.description: Event with voltage 58V triggers R-05 WARNING

testCases.name: All rules pass scenario
testCases.description: Valid telemetry event passes all 8 rules with PASS gate

testCases.name: Multiple rules fail
testCases.description: HARD_FAIL from one rule overrides WARNING from another

testCases.name: R-08 impossible telemetry
testCases.description: SOC increase > 5% without charging source triggers R-08

securityRequirements: No real security events or incident data used
Rule engine logic is read-only (no state mutation)
All outputs tagged simulated=true
Input events validated against schema before evaluation
manualVerificationSteps: Evaluate event with null timestamp and verify R-02 HARD_FAIL
Evaluate event with voltage 65V and verify R-05 WARNING or HARD_FAIL
Evaluate perfect event and verify PASS on all 8 rules
Launch Streamlit and test rule evaluation UI
Call POST /evaluate API and verify correct gate decision
definitionOfDone: All 8 rules implemented with correct logic and thresholds

name: EV Battery Digital Twin Simulator
purpose: Create a lightweight v1 digital twin of a battery that tracks identity, telemetry, health, trust score, event history, and mission readiness state.
businessGoal: Provide a single stateful representation of a battery that aggregates data from all other simulators, enabling holistic battery state visibility for mission planning.
problemStatement: Battery state is spread across identity, telemetry, health, and cybersecurity simulators. A digital twin aggregates these into a single consistent view.

simulates: Battery identity state (BPAN, certificate status, owner)
Current telemetry state (SOH, SOC, temperature, voltage)
Health state (degradation trend, cycle count, anomalies)
Trust score state (current score, readiness band, last evaluation)
Event history (last N events with timestamps)
Mission readiness state (READY / CAUTION / GROUNDED)
inputs: Snapshot data from SIM-001, SIM-002, SIM-003, SIM-004, SIM-008
assumptions: Twin is built from simulator outputs, not real hardware data
limitations: v1 only — no real-time sync or physics model

functionalRequirements: Create digital twin from battery pack data (SIM-001 output)
Attach identity state from SIM-002 output
Attach latest telemetry state from SIM-003 output
Attach trust score and readiness band from SIM-004 output
Attach health state from SIM-008 output
Maintain event history (last 20 events)
Determine and return overall mission readiness (READY / CAUTION / GROUNDED)
nonFunctionalRequirements: Lightweight, stateless snapshot model for v1
CLI for creating and displaying twin snapshot
Streamlit dashboard for twin state visualisation
FastAPI for twin creation and query
architectureNotes: DigitalTwinBuilder aggregates outputs from simulators 001–004 and 008 into a unified TwinSnapshot. Streamlit renders a dashboard with tabs for each twin state dimension.

apiEndpoints.description: Build digital twin snapshot from simulator inputs
Return last built twin snapshot for battery
Health check

testCases.name: Twin build from all simulators
testCases.description: Build twin from all 5 simulator outputs and verify all state sections present

testCases.name: Mission readiness READY
testCases.description: Trust score ≥90 and valid identity returns READY readiness

testCases.name: Mission readiness GROUNDED
testCases.description: Trust score ≤39 returns GROUNDED readiness

testCases.name: Event history limit
testCases.description: Twin stores at most last 20 events

securityRequirements: Twin contains only simulated data
All outputs tagged simulated=true
No real device state stored or transmitted
Twin data not persisted beyond current session in v1
manualVerificationSteps: Build twin from SIM-001 output and verify all state sections populated
Verify mission readiness changes with different trust score inputs
Launch Streamlit dashboard and verify twin state tabs display correctly
Call POST /build-twin API and verify complete snapshot in response
Run pytest and verify integration tests pass
definitionOfDone: Streamlit dashboard with all state tabs

name: Battery Health / SOH-SOC Simulator
purpose: Simulate battery health, state of health (SOH), state of charge (SOC), degradation patterns, cycle count, and anomaly conditions.
businessGoal: Provide realistic battery health data for testing SOH/SOC dependent logic in trust scores, registry filtering, and health-based mission readiness decisions.
problemStatement: SOH and SOC data must cover normal operation as well as degraded, anomalous, and critical health states. Real battery degradation data is not available during development.

simulates: Normal degradation (linear SOH decline with cycle count)
Rapid degradation (accelerated SOH decline scenario)
Cell imbalance (uneven SOC across cells)
Thermal stress (high temperature impact on SOH)
Low SOH alert scenario (SOH < 70%)
High internal resistance scenario
assumptions: Linear degradation model for v1
SOH decreases approximately 0.02% per cycle
limitations: Does not model capacity fade accurately for all chemistries

functionalRequirements: Calculate SOH based on cycle count using simplified linear degradation model
Simulate normal degradation (0.02% SOH loss per cycle)
Simulate rapid degradation (0.1% SOH loss per cycle)
Simulate cell imbalance: individual cell SOC spread > 10%
Simulate thermal stress: SOH reduction due to high temperature events
Generate health anomaly list for low SOH, high internal resistance, cell imbalance
Return health grade: EXCELLENT (>90%), GOOD (75–90%), FAIR (60–74%), POOR (<60%)
nonFunctionalRequirements: FastAPI REST API for integration with trust score simulator
architectureNotes: HealthScenarioEngine selects degradation model, computes SOH/SOC, assigns health grade, and generates anomaly list. SIM-008 is consumed directly by SIM-004 (trust score) and SIM-007 (digital twin).

apiEndpoints.description: Generate health snapshot for a given battery and scenario
List health simulation scenarios
Health check

testCases.name: Normal degradation at cycle 500
testCases.description: Verify SOH ≈ 90% at 500 cycles with 0.02% loss per cycle

testCases.name: Rapid degradation at cycle 200
testCases.description: Verify SOH ≈ 80% at 200 cycles with 0.1% loss per cycle

testCases.name: Cell imbalance anomaly
testCases.description: Cell imbalance scenario returns CELL_IMBALANCE anomaly

testCases.name: Health grade boundaries
testCases.description: SOH=91% returns EXCELLENT, SOH=74% returns FAIR

testCases.name: Low SOH alert
testCases.description: SOH < 60% triggers POOR grade and LOW_SOH anomaly

securityRequirements: No real battery health data used
All outputs tagged simulated=true
Degradation models clearly documented as simplified approximations
manualVerificationSteps: Run normal degradation for 1000 cycles and verify SOH ≈ 80%
Run cell imbalance scenario and verify CELL_IMBALANCE anomaly
Launch Streamlit and verify degradation curve chart
Call POST /health-snapshot and verify health grade and anomalies
Run pytest and verify all tests pass
definitionOfDone: Health grade correctly assigned by SOH range
Anomaly detection for low SOH, cell imbalance, high resistance

name: Audit & Evidence Simulator
purpose: Generate audit events, verification evidence packages, report metadata, and traceability records for battery identity and cybersecurity operations.
businessGoal: Provide automated evidence generation for QA and architect review processes, reducing manual evidence collection overhead and ensuring traceable, verifiable records.
problemStatement: Each story requires substantial evidence collection (architecture docs, test reports, screenshots, audit logs). Without a simulator, evidence generation is manual and error-prone.

simulates: User action audit log (login, query, verify, transfer events)
Simulation run log (each simulator execution record)
Evidence checklist generation (per story or simulator)
Verification status report (pass/fail per checklist item)
Report package metadata (title, date, reviewer, status)
Demo evidence metadata (video link, presentation link, screenshots)
assumptions: All evidence is simulated or manually provided
Verification status is manually set

functionalRequirements: Generate user action audit log with event type, actor, timestamp, and outcome
Generate simulation run log entry per simulator execution
Generate evidence checklist for a given story ID with all required evidence fields
Return checklist with MISSING / SUBMITTED / APPROVED status per field
Generate verification status report from checklist responses
Generate report package metadata: title, date, reviewer, status, artifact links
Generate demo evidence metadata: demo video link, presentation, screenshots count
nonFunctionalRequirements: Streamlit UI for checklist completion and report generation
FastAPI for programmatic report package creation
All outputs tagged simulated=true or generated_by=SIM-009
architectureNotes: AuditEventFactory generates audit events. EvidenceChecklistEngine generates per-story checklists. VerificationReportBuilder compiles verification results into report packages. Streamlit provides checklist UI.
cliCommands: python -m sim_009 audit-log --actor developer@intern --event verify_identity --outcome PASS

apiEndpoints.description: Generate evidence checklist for a story or simulator
Log an audit event
Generate verification report from checklist responses
Health check

testCases.name: Evidence checklist generation
testCases.description: Checklist for WP-001-S1 contains all 14+ required evidence fields

testCases.name: Completion percentage calculation
testCases.description: 5 of 14 fields SUBMITTED returns completion_percent = 35.7%

testCases.name: Audit log event creation
testCases.description: Audit event includes actor, event_type, timestamp, and outcome

testCases.name: Verification report from checklist
testCases.description: Report builder returns pass/fail per checklist item

securityRequirements: No real personal or organisational data used in sample logs
Simulated audit events clearly tagged generated_by=SIM-009
All outputs tagged simulated=true
No evidence links auto-validated (manual entry only)
manualVerificationSteps: Generate checklist for WP-001-S1 and verify all evidence fields present
Log an audit event and verify timestamp, actor, and outcome fields
Launch Streamlit and verify checklist UI with status dropdown per field
Call POST /generate-checklist API and verify response
Run pytest and verify all tests pass
definitionOfDone: Checklist generation working for all 10 stories and 10 simulators

name: Integration Test Harness Simulator
purpose: Run end-to-end test scenarios across all simulators, validating the complete Battery Trust Platform pipeline from battery pack to final trust score and evidence report.
businessGoal: Provide a single orchestration tool that exercises the entire simulator ecosystem, demonstrating cross-simulator integration and generating final demo evidence.
problemStatement: With 10 independent simulators, integration testing requires a harness that chains them together in the correct sequence to validate the full system flow.

simulates: Full pipeline: Battery Pack → Identity Verification → Telemetry → Detection Rules → Cyber Attack → Trust Score → Evidence Report
Scenario: happy path — all checks pass, trust score ≥90
Scenario: attack detected — replay attack triggers hard-fail gate
Scenario: degraded battery — low SOH reduces trust score to CAUTION
Scenario: identity failure — expired certificate blocks mission readiness
doesNotSimulate: Real system integration or production APIs
assumptions: All simulators are available locally
Simulators are called as Python modules (not HTTP)
limitations: Requires all simulators installed in same environment

functionalRequirements: Orchestrate SIM-001 to generate battery pack
Call SIM-002 for identity verification with configurable scenario
Call SIM-003 to generate telemetry events
Call SIM-006 to run detection rules on telemetry events
Call SIM-005 to simulate configurable cyber attack
Call SIM-004 to compute trust score from all inputs
Call SIM-009 to generate evidence report package
Return complete end-to-end test result with per-step status
nonFunctionalRequirements: Orchestrates all simulators in one command
Streamlit UI for scenario selection and pipeline visualisation
architectureNotes: PipelineOrchestrator runs each simulator in sequence, passing outputs between steps. Pipeline state is tracked as a StepResult list. Streamlit renders a pipeline flow diagram with per-step status.

apiEndpoints.description: Run end-to-end integration test pipeline for a scenario
List available integration test scenarios
Return result from last pipeline run
Health check

testCases.name: Happy path pipeline
testCases.description: Full pipeline with valid identity and normal telemetry returns PASSED and MISSION_READY

testCases.name: Attack detected pipeline
testCases.description: Replay attack triggers R-01 HARD_FAIL gate and trust score drops to GROUNDED

testCases.name: Identity failure pipeline
testCases.description: Expired certificate causes identity EXPIRED and trust score penalty

testCases.name: All 6 steps complete
testCases.description: Every pipeline run returns exactly 6 step results with simulator IDs

testCases.name: Reproducibility with seed
testCases.description: Same seed and scenario produces identical pipeline output

securityRequirements: Integration tests use only simulated data
All pipeline outputs tagged simulated=true
No real system connections in any pipeline step
Attack scenarios clearly marked SIMULATED_ATTACK
manualVerificationSteps: Run happy_path scenario and verify MISSION_READY outcome
Run attack_detected scenario and verify HARD_FAIL gate and lower trust score
Launch Streamlit and verify pipeline flow diagram with step statuses
Call POST /run-pipeline API and verify step results in response
Run pytest integration tests and verify all 4 scenarios tested
definitionOfDone: All 6 pipeline steps orchestrated in correct sequence
4 integration scenarios (happy path, attack, degraded, identity failure) working
Streamlit pipeline flow diagram UI functional
